description:
Validate the ergonomics of the new manifest + memoization path by porting one real pipeline: sdf-hallucination's eval chain (sample → classify → plot). Chosen because it's the cleanest staged pipeline of the six audited repos and currently has the silent-overwrite bug that memoization kills (run_refclass_eval.py:97 overwrites raw responses unconditionally). Success = the port is less code than the original scripts, re-runs are free, figures regenerate from artifacts, and the manifest answers 'which commit produced this figure'. Friction found here feeds back into stg-teu.1/stg-teu.2 before wider migration.

## Acceptance criteria
sdf-hallucination eval chain runs as a stagehand Flow; re-run with unchanged inputs does zero API calls; each output artifact traceable to git SHA + config; write-up of ergonomic friction filed against the parent issues.

## Notes
Pilot implemented + validated live → https://github.com/ArcadiaImpact/sdf-hallucination/pull/5 (scripts/refclass_flow.py). Acceptance met: identical re-run = zero API calls (8s replay), replay works with NO keys in env, manifest answers which-commit, --smoke runs whole DAG into results/smoke/. Close on merge.
title: Pilot: port sdf-hallucination eval chain to a stagehand Flow
